div.head: A.
div.p: Sir: With reference to your letter of the 16th instant, relative to the case of Edward McGingan, at present confined in Mountjoy prison, I am directed by the lord lieutenant to acquaint you that his excellency has reason to believe that this man is a natural-born subject of the Queen, and that he does not feel it consistent with his duty to ralease him from custody. I am to add that, in giving you this reply as a matter of courtesy, his excellency thinks it right to point out that no evidence is adduced that McGingan is a citizen of the United States, either natural-born or naturalized; and that in the case of naturalized United States citizens the production of their papers of naturalization is the only evidence which can be accepted as satisfactory.

postscript.p: W. B. West, Esq., United States Consul, Dublin, Ireland.
